ref: ‚ÄúNo!‚Äù the Lord answered. ‚ÄúAnyone who kills you will be punished seven times worse than I am punishing you.‚Äù So the Lord put a mark on Cain to warn everyone not to kill him.
text: Na ∆êd…õnk…õma zele ye k…õ, ‚ÄúKy…õky…õ, …înle z…îhane! Awie biala m…î…î k…õhu Keen la, b…õbali ye nzenral…õ fane nsuu b…õazie …î bo zo.‚Äù Na ∆êd…õnk…õma y…õle Keen nzonl…õ, amaa awie biala m…î…î nee ye k…õyia la angu ye.
translated: And Edinkemah said to them, ‚ÄúListen, people, let's go! Those who see from Keen's place, do not let your homesteads be near bad water along the way we go.‚Äù And Edinkemah sent Keen to go, and those who see the path that they follow.

text: Yem…îti …îmanle b…õvale niny…õne ne m…î b…õlumuale …î nyunlu, na yem…î …îhale azoko…õle…õ ne kenle ko ne n…îe ne. Gyek…îbo futu w…î P…õne…õle

text: Akee Leeban zele Gyek…îbo k…õ, ‚ÄúMe busuanli a le w…î suzuamgba …õde…õ, noko …înle k…õ …õy…õ gyima …õmaa me m…î…î mendua w…î kak…õ a. Ka m…î…î kenle ko …õk…õlie la kile me.‚Äù
translated: Chief Akee Leeban said to Gyekobo, "Our market people go to the susu house every day, but the owner has no money so that I can take my goods there. Let me take what is not delayed and help me."

ref: Finally, God remembered Rachel‚Äîhe answered her prayer by giving her a son. ‚ÄúGod has taken away my disgrace,‚Äù she said.
text: ∆Ünrenz…õle na …îwole ral…õ nrenya. Na …îhanle k…õ, ‚ÄúNyamenle …õkpa me nyiane.‚Äù
translated: People gather and share wealth among themselves. And the chief says, ‚ÄúMay God grant us peace.‚Äù

text: Yem…îti L…î…îto vindele n…îe ne h…îdunwuele …î mra mraal…õ ne m…î b…õ hu m…î m…î…î baragya b…õ la, na …îzele b…õ k…õ, ‚ÄúB…õdwazo, b…õvi suakpole ye azo b…õye b…õ nwo, …îluak…õ ∆êd…õnk…õma …õl…õba araz…õkye suakpole ye.‚Äù Noko b…õdwenlenle k…õ …îl…õb…î nz…õmed…õ.
translated: It is said that when people gather to listen to stories and falsehoods, and we must not spread rumors here; and the elders say, "Listen well, those who spread gossip cause harm to you‚Äîknow that the community is destroyed by the gossip they spread." Therefore, we should be cautious in our actions with wisdom.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Yem…îti Nyamenle b…île sonla k…õ …îdaye ye …õsaso la; …îdaye Nyamenle mumua ne ye …õsaso a …înleanle …îb…île sonla a. ∆Üb…île b…õ nrenya nee raal…õ.
translated: They say Nyamenle prays daily for their work; every day Nyamenle provides and their work is enough for those who pray to God. Prayer is strength with great power.

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Yem…îti fa g…îfa baka bob…î …õl…õnemgbole; ky…õ nuhua ekpumgbunli ekpumgbunli na fa bilewu ka nuhua nee nwol…õ amuala.
translated: The people gather at the market every day to speak to the chief; and they listen to stories, stories, and make promises so that they are heard and work together.

ref: I will give you and them the land in which you are now a foreigner. I will give the whole land of Canaan to your family forever, and I will be their God.
text: Na mebava az…õl…õ ne m…î…î …õl…õkp…îsakp…îsa zol…õ la meamaa w…î nee …õ bo zo amra. B…õbava Keena az…õl…õ ne k…îs…î…îti b…õay…õ b…õ …õde…õ, na mebay…õ b…õ Nyamenle.‚Äù
translated: And our people say that the chief who comes to our homes with empty hands is one who does not know the way. Also, the elders say that the words they speak are true, and what we say is from Nyamenle.

ref: You didn't have much before I came, but the Lord has blessed everything I have ever done for you. Now it's time for me to start looking out for my own family.
text: ∆Üluak…õ …õnee w…î ny…õmoa le ekyi bie y…õ…õ membale a, k…õkala b…õ nwo …õzo d…î…înwo kpal…õ, y…õ…õ medame me ti, ∆êd…õnk…õma …õyila w…î w…î debie biala anu. Na k…õkala menea a akee yedwu mek…õ m…î…î …îw…î k…õ medwenle me sua nu amra anwo a.‚Äù
translated: ∆Üluak…õ, those who are at the market and do not come have problems here; also, they take their words seriously, and there are many people. Ed…õnk…õma is lying here. And also, what they are doing causes people to see with disrespectful eyes, never giving their good things.

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
text: Na m…î…î nrenya ne nwunle k…õ yeangola yeangomo Gyek…îbo anwo zo la, …îvale …î sa …îhanle Gyek…îbo …õz…îl…õ, na Gyek…îbo adwongu huanenle w…î …õfutul…õ ne anu.
translated: And the people and leaders who live in the villages around Gyek…îbo say that their work for Gyek…îbo is hard, and Gyek…îbo has many paths where people suffer and endure pain.

ref: Hagar thought, ‚ÄúHave I really seen God and lived to tell about it?‚Äù So from then on she called him, ‚ÄúThe God Who Sees Me.‚Äù
text: Na Heega hanle k…õ, ‚ÄúAsoo amgba menwu Nyamenle anyunlu na met…õte aze?‚Äù Na …îv…õl…õle ∆êd…õnk…õma m…î…î nee ye dend…õle la, ‚ÄúNyamenle m…î…î nwu me la.‚Äù
translated: Then Heega asked, ‚ÄúWhat do the people of Nyamenle here do with the council meetings?‚Äù Then the elder ∆êd…õnk…õma said clearly from here, ‚ÄúNyamenle is protecting us here.‚Äù

ref: Then he said, ‚ÄúPlease tell me who your father is. Does he have room in his house for me and my men to spend the night?‚Äù
text: Na …îzele k…õ, ‚Äú∆ê ze a le nwane? Ka kile me. Asoo …õ ze l…õ sua w…î …õk…õ ne m…î…î y…õkola y…õbasoko…õ nu a?‚Äù
translated: Then the chief said, ‚ÄúWhere do we live? Tell them about me. Also, where is the place with houses and cocoa fields for us to trade?‚Äù

ref: The Lord was standing beside the ladder and said: I am the Lord God who was worshiped by Abraham and Isaac. I will give to you and your family the land on which you are now sleeping.
text: ∆êd…õnk…õma gyi …î ti zo l…î na …îl…õka k…õ, ‚ÄúMame a mele ∆êd…õnk…õma, …õ nlenya Ebileham nee …õ ze Ayezeke Nyamenle ne a. Az…õl…õ ne m…î…î …õla zol…õ la, mebava meamaa w…î nee …õ bo zo amra.
translated: Edenkema, those who came to inquire and the chief said, ‚ÄúMother who loves Edenkema, I know Ebileham and I call Ayezeke Nyamenle and us. Let us prepare and gather the necessary things, provide us with enough so that we may go.‚Äù

ref: Noah built an altar where he could offer sacrifices to the Lord. Then he offered on the altar one of each kind of animal and bird that could be used for a sacrifice.
text: Nowa zile azukponle manle ∆êd…õnk…õma. Akee …îhyele nane nee nloma ne m…î m…î…î anwo te la biala anu ko, …îhunle b…õ na …îyelale b…õ …îb…île azu w…î azukponle ne azo …îmanle Nyamenle.
translated: Nowadays, the youth and the people of Ed…õnk…õma are discussing. They say the matter is important and that they are not doing good, that they do not know how to behave properly, because the elders are no longer able to guide the youth and uphold the traditions of the Nyamenle people.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Yem…îti Nyamenle b…île sonla k…õ …îdaye ye …õsaso la; …îdaye Nyamenle mumua ne ye …õsaso a …înleanle …îb…île sonla a. ∆Üb…île b…õ nrenya nee raal…õ.
translated: They say Nyamenle prays for rain every day for their crops; but every day, Nyamenle and their crops, the farmers pray for rain. Their prayers are not heard or answered.

text: Yem…îti fa g…îfa baka bob…î …õl…õnemgbole; ky…õ nuhua ekpumgbunli ekpumgbunli na fa bilewu ka nuhua nee nwol…õ amuala.
translated: The people say that it is not true; listen to us, but those who talk repeatedly tell lies so that they may make the people confused.

ref: I will give you and them the land in which you are now a foreigner. I will give the whole land of Canaan to your family forever, and I will be their God.
text: Na mebava az…õl…õ ne m…î…î …õl…õkp…îsakp…îsa zol…õ la meamaa w…î nee …õ bo zo amra. B…õbava Keena az…õl…õ ne k…îs…î…îti b…õay…õ b…õ …õde…õ, na mebay…õ b…õ Nyamenle.‚Äù
translated: And the people say that those who constantly deceive with their hearts cannot find peace. But the chief says that the customs they have are good, and we know that it is God.

ref: You didn't have much before I came, but the Lord has blessed everything I have ever done for you. Now it's time for me to start looking out for my own family.
text: ∆Üluak…õ …õnee w…î ny…õmoa le ekyi bie y…õ…õ membale a, k…õkala b…õ nwo …õzo d…î…înwo kpal…õ, y…õ…õ medame me ti, ∆êd…õnk…õma …õyila w…î w…î debie biala anu. Na k…õkala menea a akee yedwu mek…õ m…î…î …îw…î k…õ medwenle me sua nu amra anwo a.‚Äù
translated: Oluak…õ, those in authority do not speak the truth here; instead, their words are filled with lies, empty and constant. The people see much conflict among themselves, and instead, what they do is bring violence to the people and suffering and death upon our land.

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
